categories.label: Array / Hash / Intervals
categories.rationale: Single-pass, hash-map, sorting, and interval merge patterns; catches basic translation and boundary handling.

categories.label: Dynamic Programming / Recursion
categories.rationale: Tests subproblem framing, recurrence extraction, base cases, and memo/table discipline.

categories.label: Two-pointer / Sliding Window
categories.rationale: Pointer movement, window invariants, and off-by-one failures.

categories.label: Graph / Tree / BFS / DFS
categories.rationale: Traversal state, queue/stack correctness, tree mutation, visited tracking.

categories.label: Stack / Queue / Data Structure
categories.rationale: Stateful APIs and operation-sequence correctness, where scaffold signatures matter.

categories.label: Strings / Grids / Constraint Checking
categories.rationale: Parsing, indexing, grid invariants, and constraint validation.

difficultyBuckets.calibration: Published easy label or expected reference solution under ~25 LOC with one dominant invariant.
Published medium label or expected reference solution ~25-60 LOC with multiple branches/invariants.
Published hard label or expected reference solution >60 LOC, multi-phase DP/search, or stateful API edge cases.

sourcingRules: Do not source expanded held-out problems from the existing testcases/ corpus or any co-listed training pool.
Each selected problem must be committed with task text, reference TypeScript signature/solution, deterministic tests, and source provenance before model eval.
Incomplete disk directories may only become held-out candidates after completion and contamination checks; until then they are tracked as incomplete, not selected.
Freeze the manifest and hashes before running raw_base, gen0_seed, or gen18_evolved.

freezeRules.rule: Compute sha256 over sorted per-problem hashes and store it in the final held-out manifest.
Any task/reference/test edit after freeze creates a new datasetVersion; no silent mutation.
Tag the freeze commit as heldout-v1-YYYYMMDD before any model eval.
Every result artifact must reference this dataset and use exactly raw_base, gen0_seed, gen18_evolved unless separately preregistered.

preregisteredPredictions.claim: gen18_evolved pass@1 exceeds raw_base on the frozen held-out set.
preregisteredPredictions.successCriterion: Positive paired difference with exact McNemar p < 0.05; report Bonferroni-adjusted interpretation across preregistered claims.

preregisteredPredictions.claim: gen18_evolved pass@1 exceeds gen0_seed, but by less than the N=4 50-point apparent gap.
gen18 advantage is larger on medium+hard buckets than easy bucket.
preregisteredPredictions.successCriterion: (medium_hard gap) > (easy gap) for gen18_evolved minus raw_base.

preregisteredPredictions.claim: gen0_seed improves pass@N recovery more than pass@1.
preregisteredPredictions.successCriterion: gen0_seed pass@N - raw_base pass@N > gen0_seed pass@1 - raw_base pass@1.

preregisteredPredictions.claim: Dominant failures cluster in logic_assertion and spec_validation sub-kinds, not model_error.
preregisteredPredictions.successCriterion: At least 80% of failed attempts have failureKind in {logic_assertion, spec_validation, format_protocol}; model_error remains below 10%.

secondaryMetrics: bucketed pass@1 by difficulty and category
statisticalPlan.multipleComparisonNote: Preregistered predictions are interpreted with both raw p-values and Bonferroni-adjusted p<0.01 sensitivity notes.

approvalGate.reason: Expanded held-out evaluation can consume paid/remote model calls and changes evidential status; freeze and approval precede runs.
